Quarterly Reportage

3993 key announcements across 39 quarters (2017Q1 → today), each mapped to its scheme and ministry from the PIB register. Generated 2026-07-22 by scripts/build_reportage.py — keyword mapping is an index into the register, not a substitute for reading the release. Quarters are calendar quarters (2026Q3 = Jul–Sep 2026). Gap-audited 21-Jul-2026 against Drishti IAS, Vision IAS and all 18 PRS Monthly Policy Reviews (Jan-25→Jun-26): full-title scan + 30 new scheme families added; pre-audit coverage was ~50% of PRS-recorded incentive actions. Companion view: ministry-wise reportage (grouped by owning ministry, rows time-stamped newest-first). Part of digital-twin-for-ipa.

State-level incentives — status & funds (public records)

Disbursals are distinguished from approvals/LoCs/MoUs; arrears are the key performance signal. "UNVERIFIED" and absence of published data are recorded as findings. Sources: government releases, state budgets, PIB/Parliament replies, business press.

ARREARS DIVIDE: Telangana Rs 3,736cr confirmed arrears (overlapping counts to >Rs 4,500cr since 2016/17); AP Rs 3,000-5,000cr pending (YSRCP-era disbursal ~Rs 2,000cr/5y); Punjab's 'Rs 150cr Q1 FY26 exceeded prior two years combined' is an implicit arrears admission; J&K NCSS approved ~Rs 83cr in FY24 against Rs 28,400cr outlay with documented industry delay complaints.

TRANSPARENCY DIVIDE: Rajasthan press-releases annual RIPS disbursals (Rs 765.78cr FY25, +293%); Gujarat publishes MSME-track numbers (Rs 7,864cr/5y) but gates the Large/Mega dashboard behind login; Maharashtra and Karnataka publish NO annual disbursal series; Haryana and West Bengal have no findable aggregate outgo.

OUTLIERS: MP actively disburses on-stage (Rs 270cr+425cr at one conclave; claims liabilities cleared); Odisha budgets only Rs 15cr/yr for IPR subsidies against a Rs 24,823cr MoU pipeline; Assam's semiconductor state share is Rs 3,789cr of the Rs 14,044cr Tata OSAT stack.

Gujarat · 6 catalogued instruments

Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Schemes for Assistance to Industries 2022 (MSME / Large / M
ACTIVE - operative 5 Oct 2022 to Oct 2027 (replaced Industrial Policy 2020 incentive schemes); disbursals ongoing alongside legacy 2015 and 2020 policy claims
Rs 245 crore to ~2,400 enterprises — Aatmanirbhar Gujarat scheme (launched 2022) has provided assistance to enterprises since inception (MSME track) [govt-release]
Rs 7,864 crore to ~1.3 lakh MSMEs — Total MSME industrial assistance disbursed by Gujarat govt FY2020-21 to FY2024-25 (capital subsidy, interest subvention, credit guarantee across policy frameworks) [govt-release]
Rs 345 cr (IP-2020) + Rs 137 cr (IP-2015) — Legacy scheme disbursals still running in parallel: Industrial Policy 2020 claims Rs 345 crore across 8,700 claims; Industrial Policy 2015 claims Rs 137 crore across 4,400 claims (1 Apr 2024 - 8 Jan 2 [govt-release]
UNVERIFIED (dashboard login-gated) — IFP (Investor Facilitation Portal) has an 'Incentives Granted Dashboard' menu item, but no numbers are publicly visible without login - Large/Mega-track disbursal totals UNVERIFIED from public web [govt-release]
News: DeshGujarat (carrying Gujarat govt statement, Gandhinagar) (2025-05-16) · CMO Gujarat (official) (2022-10-05)

Maharashtra · 6 catalogued instruments

Package Scheme of Incentives PSI-2019 / Maharashtra Industry, Investment & Servi
TRANSITION - PSI-2019 window closed for new units; MIISP-2025 announced with FY26 budget (target Rs 40 lakh crore investment, 50 lakh jobs); PSI-2019 committed disbursals continue via cabinet sub-committee / High Power Committee approvals
Rs 89,731 crore investment approved, ~20,000 jobs — Cabinet sub-committee chaired by CM Fadnavis approved 5 mega/ultra-mega high-tech project proposals under the industrial promotion policy (incl. Adani Enterprises Rs 70,000 cr coal gasification Nagpur [news]
Rs 1.35 lakh crore approvals — State later approved Rs 1.35 lakh crore of high-tech sector investment proposals (incentives under mega-project provisions) expected to create ~1 lakh jobs [govt-release]
UNVERIFIED (disbursal outgo not published in accessible summary form) — Actual PSI/Industrial Promotion Subsidy disbursal (outgo) line for FY2024-25/FY2025-26 not found in public web sources; PRS budget analysis of Maharashtra Budget 2025-26 (total expenditure Rs 7,00,020 [budget-doc]
News: Deccan Chronicle (2025) · PRS Legislative Research (2025-03) · KRPR & Associates (practitioner analysis) (2025)

Tamil Nadu · 9 catalogued instruments

Industrial Policy 2021 structured packages (Mega/Super-Mega/Ultra-Mega investmen
ACTIVE - structured packages continue under Industrial Policy 2021; FY26 budget carries an explicit Investment Promotion Incentive allocation; MoU pipeline actively converting
Rs 2,420 crore (FY2025-26 BE) — Tamil Nadu Budget 2025-26 allocates an 'Investment Promotion Incentive for Industries' line (structured-package subsidy outgo) plus Rs 225 cr for Women Entrepreneurship Development Scheme [budget-doc]
Rs 2.07 lakh crore (270 MoUs, 2025) — Investment commitments received by TN in calendar 2025 via Guidance TN: 270 MoUs signed, expected to generate over 4 lakh jobs (Minister TRB Rajaa) [news]
Rs 6.64 lakh crore MoUs, ~26.90 lakh jobs claimed — Global Investors Meet 2024 (Guidance TN) investment commitments announced by CM Stalin [news]
News: Business Standard (also PTI via The Week, Outlook Business) (2026-01-02) · Deccan Herald (2024-01)

Karnataka · 7 catalogued instruments

Karnataka Industrial Policy 2020-25 (expired) / Industrial Policy 2025-30 incent
ACTIVE - Industrial Policy 2025-30 notified 8 Feb 2025 (5-year validity; target Rs 7.5 lakh cr investment, 20 lakh jobs); 2020-25 policy commitments in disbursal tail; incentives are backend-loaded (released post-production/employment milestones)
Rs 10,980 crore over 3 years — Karnataka offered incentives and concessions worth at least Rs 10,980 crore to companies over the preceding three years; Industries Minister M B Patil noted bespoke incentives normally go to companies [news]
Policy terms (not outgo) — Industrial Policy 2025-30 incentive structure: capital subsidy 10-25% of fixed assets OR production-linked subsidy 1.75-2.25% of turnover; investments above Rs 1,000 cr get case-by-case customised pac [govt-release]
UNVERIFIED (no public annual disbursal series found) — Year-wise actual incentive disbursal under Industrial Policy 2020-25 for FY2024-25/FY2025-26 not published in accessible budget summaries or news [news]
News: Deccan Herald (2026-06 (a) · Grant Thornton Bharat (tax alert on official notification) (2025-02)

Uttar Pradesh · 8 catalogued instruments

Industrial Investment & Employment Promotion Policy (IIEPP) 2022 - Invest UP Let
ACTIVE - IIEPP-2022 rules (Apr 2023) in force; High Level Empowered Committee (HLEC) issuing Letters of Comfort and CM-led disbursement events through Sep 2025
Rs 164.91 crore to 17 units — CM-presided event distributed incentives to 17 industrial units under two policies (incl. IIEPP-2022); beneficiaries included CP Milk, Omax Auto, Ambuja Cement, Suyash Paper Mill, Birla Corporation, V [govt-release]
76 LoCs, ~Rs 19,115 crore investment — Cumulative LoC issuance reported by Invest UP: 40 Mega/Mega-Plus/Super-Mega projects (investment ~Rs 17,113.79 cr, 19,750 jobs) and 36 large-category projects (~Rs 2,001.60 cr, 7,076 jobs) [govt-release]
Rs 576.78 crore (6 proposals) — HLEC cleared 6 proposals for Letters of Comfort under IIEPP-2022 [govt-release]

Rajasthan · 2 catalogued instruments

Rajasthan Investment Promotion Scheme - RIPS-2019/2022 (legacy claims) and RIPS-
ACTIVE - RIPS-2024 in force (updated 16 May 2025); record disbursal year FY2024-25; approvals pipeline continuing into FY2025-26; LaabhCalc incentive calculator launched
Rs 765.78 crore (FY2024-25) vs Rs 261 crore (FY2023-24) — Industries Department disbursed highest-ever RIPS incentives in FY2024-25, a 293% jump over Rs 261 crore in FY2023-24; manufacturing, renewable energy and MSME projects took the bulk [news]
Rs 765 crore, +293% YoY — Official confirmation of the FY25 record disbursal by BIP Rajasthan (Rising Rajasthan handle) [govt-release]
75+ proposals — Over 75 proposals approved under RIPS in a subsequent approvals round (approvals count, FY2025-26 cycle) [news]
Scheme text (updated compendium) — RIPS-2024 consolidated scheme document (incl. customs-duty/SGST-linked benefit heads) published by Finance (Tax) Department [budget-doc]

Telangana · 9 catalogued instruments

T-IDEA / T-PRIDE (Telangana State Industrial Development and Entrepreneur Advanc arrears signal
Schemes operative since 2014 (now supplemented by TGFPS portal); large multi-year arrears confirmed; Congress govt (Dec 2023-) clearing dues in phased tranches through FY2025-26
Rs 3,736 crore total arrears — BRS government left industrial-incentive arrears when it demitted office Dec 2023: Rs 3,007 cr to medium/small-scale industries + Rs 728 cr to mega industries; Rs 684 cr of FY2023-24 cheques lapsed un [news]
Rs 400-500 crore tranche; dues pending since 2014 — Industries & IT Minister D. Sridhar Babu announced disbursal of Rs 400-500 crore in industrial subsidies by end-March 2025, addressing dues pending since 2014 [news]
Rs 2,200 crore SC/ST dues; >Rs 4,500 crore total since 2017 — SC and ST entrepreneurs awaiting Rs 2,200 crore in industrial subsidies pending from 2017; total unpaid industrial subsidies since 2017 reported at over Rs 4,500 crore; first tranche of Rs 300 crore p [news]
Rs 4,250 crore (UNVERIFIED against primary source) — Separate reported figure: pending incentives to industries since 2016 stated as Rs 4,250 crore (search-snippet figure, not independently fetched; overlaps/conflicts with Rs 3,736 cr and Rs 4,500 cr fi [news]

Andhra Pradesh · 6 catalogued instruments

AP Industrial Development Policy 4.0 (2024-29) + legacy pending incentives from arrears signal
IDP 4.0 notified Nov 2024 (targets incl. Rs 83,000 cr FDI); TDP-led govt acknowledges large backlog of unpaid incentive claims from YSRCP period and has committed to phased FIFO release
Rs 3,000-5,000 crore pending; YSRCP-era disbursal ~Rs 2,000 crore in 5 years — Industries Minister T G Bharath: government will 'soon' release pending incentives ranging Rs 3,000-5,000 crore to entrepreneurs on first-in-first-out basis; YSRCP govt released only ~Rs 2,000 cr over [news]
UNVERIFIED (no exact FY2025-26 disbursal figure found) — IDP 4.0 policy document (official, apindustries.gov.in) sets 2024-29 incentive framework; no consolidated FY2025-26 disbursal-vs-arrears statement located in public budget docs during this sweep [govt-release]

Odisha · 4 catalogued instruments

Industrial Policy Resolution (IPR) 2022 incentives arrears signal
IPR-2022 operative with 2025-26 amendments (GO-EAST framework, extra incentives for 15 under-industrialised districts, Go-SWIFT tracking of incentive disbursement); budgeted subsidy outgo under IPR is strikingly small relative to MoU pipeline
Rs 15 crore (IPR subsidies, BE 2025-26) — Odisha Annual Budget 2025-26 allocates Rs 15 crore for 'Subsidies under IPR', Rs 30 crore for subsidies under MSME Policy, Rs 25 crore for Food Processing Policy subsidies (figures surfaced from finan [budget-doc]
Rs 24,823 crore MoUs (commitments, not disbursals) — At GO-EAST policy launch alongside IPR-2022 amendments, state signed MoUs with 7 corporations worth ~Rs 24,823 crore (chemicals, power, green-energy equipment, apparel, steel); Go-SWIFT to track incen [news]
UNVERIFIED — No cumulative IPR-2022 incentive disbursal total or arrears figure located in public record during this sweep [govt-release]

Madhya Pradesh · 5 catalogued instruments

Investment Promotion Assistance (IPAS/MPIS) — MP Industrial Promotion Policy 202 arrears signal
New Industrial Promotion Policy notified 2025-02-24 with Investment Promotion Scheme 2025 for large/mega units (valid for projects commencing before 2030-03-31); state actively disbursing at Regional Industry Conclave events and claims pending liabilities clea
Rs 695 crore disbursed (Rs 270 cr MSME + Rs 425 cr large) in one event — At MP-RISE 2025 conclave (Ratlam), CM Mohan Yadav disbursed Rs 270 crore incentive grants to 288 MSMEs and Rs 425 crore financial support to 140 large industries; Rs 30,402 crore investment proposals [news]
Rs 360+ crore to ~900 units; 'pending liabilities cleared' claim — CM Mohan Yadav disbursed over Rs 360 crore in incentives to ~900 MSMEs/industrial units and stated pending liabilities were cleared; ancillary releases: Rs 2.02 cr ETP assistance, Rs 1.07 cr mandi-fee [news]
framework document (no aggregate) — MP Investment Promotion Scheme 2025 (official text via NSWS): large-scale units commencing production after 2025-02-24 eligible; SLEC (State Level Empowered Committee) sanctions assistance on MPIDC sc [govt-release]

Banglashree for MSMEs (2020) + WB MSME incentive outgo arrears signal
Banglashree operative via Silpasathi single window; FY2025-26 budget carries Rs 1,229 crore MSME allocation; no consolidated public statement of annual incentive disbursal/outgo or arrears found
Rs 1,229 crore MSME allocation BE 2025-26 — WB Budget 2025-26 allocated Rs 1,229 crore for MSMEs (department allocation, not scheme-wise disbursal); state also approved a Rs 5,000 crore electronics/semiconductor/EV incentive package [news]
Rs 100 crore (FY2020-21 allocation) — Historical baseline: Banglashree budget allocation was Rs 100 crore in FY2020-21; no later scheme-wise outgo figures located [govt-release]

Punjab · 2 catalogued instruments

Industrial and Business Development Policy (IBDP) 2022 — Invest Punjab arrears signal
Policy operative; state publicly claims accelerated FY2025-26 disbursals specifically to clear past pending incentive claims — implicit admission of prior arrears backlog
Rs 150+ crore Q1 FY2025-26; Rs 250 crore target — Over Rs 150 crore disbursed in Q1 FY2025-26 — stated to exceed the combined total of the previous two years (an implicit arrears signal: prior two full years together < Rs 150 cr); target ~Rs 250 cror [news]
Rs 500 crore disbursed FY2025-26 (state claim) — State claims Rs 500 crore disbursed in 2025-26 to eligible industrial units under various state industrial policies (manufacturing, food processing, textiles, IT/ITeS, steel, auto, agro); reforms sinc [news]

Haryana · 2 catalogued instruments

Haryana Enterprises & Employment Policy (HEEP) 2020 arrears signal
Policy valid until 2025-12-31 (successor policy expected); SGST-reimbursement 30-100% for 5-10 years by block category; NO public aggregate disbursal or arrears figure located
framework only — HEEP 2020 (notified 2020-12-29) framework: investment subsidy in lieu of net SGST 30-100% reimbursement 5-10 yrs by block A/B/C/D; interest subsidy 5-8% for 5-7 yrs; policy sunset 2025-12-31 [govt-release]
Rs 170 crore (cluster plan) — 2025 announcements: Rs 170 crore MSME cluster development plan (11 clusters) and PADMA mini industrial parks across 143 blocks — allocations, not HEEP disbursals [news]
UNVERIFIED — no figure found in budget docs, CAG extracts, or news during this sweep — Total HEEP-2020 incentive disbursed to date and any pendency/arrears position [budget-doc]

Assam · 7 catalogued instruments

Industrial & Investment Policy of Assam (IIPA) 2019 (amended 2023) arrears signal
Operative with 2023 mega-investment amendment (Rs 100 cr+ investment / 200+ jobs get customised incentives); implementation sub-committee minutes public on industries dept site; no aggregate disbursal figure published
Rs 13,364 crore investments secured — Via the 2023 IIPA amendment, Assam secured investments of INR 133.64 billion (~Rs 13,364 crore) and 17,800 jobs in 14 months (commitments under customised-incentive route, not disbursals) [news]
UNVERIFIED — only sub-committee minutes (case-by-case approvals) public — Aggregate IIPA-2019 incentive disbursal / arrears figure [govt-release]
Semiconductor package (Tata Electronics OSAT, Jagiroad) + Rs 25,000 cr electroni
Rs 27,000 crore Tata OSAT plant fast-tracked for production; combined govt incentive of Rs 14,044 crore confirmed (Centre Rs 10,255 cr under India Semiconductor Mission + Assam state Rs 3,789 cr)
Rs 14,044 crore (Rs 3,789 crore state share) — Tata's Rs 27,000-crore Jagiroad (Morigaon) semiconductor ATMP plant to receive Rs 14,044 crore total government incentive: Rs 10,255 crore (₹102.55 bn) central semiconductor-programme contribution + R [news]
Rs 25,000 crore plan — Assam unveiled Rs 25,000 crore plan (2025-05-27) to boost electronics manufacturing, large portion earmarked for incentivising component makers (PCB assemblies, display modules) [news]
News: ETV Bharat (2026-07-09) · BIS Infotech (2025-26)

Jammu & Kashmir · 6 catalogued instruments

New Central Sector Scheme (NCSS) 2021 for Industrial Development of J&K (DPIIT-a
Registrations continuing (2,346 applications, 918 units granted registration per reported figures); early-year disbursals were very small relative to outlay; industry bodies publicly complaining of disbursement delays
Rs 28,400 crore outlay — Lok Sabha written reply (MoS Home Nityanand Rai): NCSS notified with Rs 28,400 crore outlay; components = Capital Investment Incentive, Capital Interest Subvention, GST-linked incentive, Working Capit [govt-release]
~Rs 82.94 crore approved 2023-24 — In 2023-24, approvals under NCSS: Rs 62.5 crore Capital Investment Incentive + Rs 19 crore Working Capital Interest Subvention + Rs 1.44 crore Capital Interest Subvention (per Lok Sabha reply as repor [govt-release]
1,948 claims disbursed; Rs 31.76 crore (pharma-sector releases) — 1,948 NCSS claims disbursed to date (1,139 Jammu + 809 Kashmir divisions); 2,346 industrial establishments applied, 918 granted registration (figures from Rising Kashmir reporting; Rs 31.76 crore figu [news]
672 units / 2 years — 672 new industrial units established in J&K in 2 years attributed to NCSS [news]
News: Daily Excelsior (UNVERIFIED) · The Week (2025-03-17) · PIB (Press Information Bureau) (2024-08)
Every PRID links to the original release on pib.gov.in. Corrections in the underlying registers are displayed, never silent. Companion views: the scheme catalogue, portal directory and leads list in the repo's docs/.

Annex — what the clearance registers add (policy findings)

1. Half of India's live industrial EC pipeline is chemicals. Of 1,384 live corporate industrial filers beyond the verified leads, 51% file under synthetic-organic-chemicals — the China import-substitution story is already the dominant physical activity in the clearance register, mostly self-funded and mostly unannounced.

2. The API localisation wave is running outside PLI. The only confirmed KSM/API-PLI beneficiary in the 98 verified leads (Orchid Pharma, ₹600 cr 7-ACA) carries the cohort's worst execution profile (land delays, downgrade), while IOL, Farmson, Anthem, Aarti, Piramal and Virupaksha localise the same China-dependent chains self-funded. Land assembly, not incentive size, is the binding constraint.

3. Verified foreign capex is visible in the registers before the press. Lanxess (DE), Pernod Ricard (FR, ₹1,785 cr Nagpur), HS Hyosung (KR, ~₹3,000 cr cumulative), Kansai Nerolac (JP), Sumitomo (JP, via TruAlt), plus SRF's Odisha filings fronting the ₹10,000 cr pledge.

4. Cross-verification: no name rests on one source. All 41 headline companies ground-truth in the raw PARIVESH registers; only 17 have ever appeared in a PIB headline — the registers see ~2.4× more of these investors than PIB headlines do. IEM Part-B company data is held for only two sample months, so absence there is weak evidence.

EC/Forest counts are ground-truthed from the raw PARIVESH registers (all years, statuses, activities) and are deliberately broader than the 2025-26 live-manufacturing subset used for lead scoring. PIB is a title-only index — annexure PDFs are a known blind spot. "Quiet" = live clearance filings with zero press. Sources & data: layers/24_clearance_leads.json · 24b_pool_policy_triage.json · 24c_cross_verification.json. Generated 2026-07-22.
